editorial.title: Critical ballot shortage failure
editorial.body: [ED] Critical ballot shortage failure The Korea Times
summary: South Korea faces a critical shortage of ballots ahead of elections.
Election officials failed to adequately prepare voting materials.
The shortage threatens smooth voting and raises election integrity concerns.
editorial.stance: Editorial condemns electoral mismanagement and demands urgent government accountability.
editorial.titleTr: 투표용지 부족 사태, 심각한 관리 실패
summaryTr: 남한이 선거를 앞두고 투표용지 심각한 부족 사태에 직면했다.
선거 관계자들이 투표 자료를 충분히 준비하지 못했다.
이번 부족 사태는 원활한 투표를 위협하며 선거 공정성에 대한 우려를 야기하고 있다.

leanScore.label: Center
leanScore.rationale: Editorial critiques government incompetence on a non-partisan issue (ballot logistics) without invoking left/right ideological frames, structural inequality arguments, or characteristic progressive/conservative policy positions.

editorial.title: 국민의힘, 지도부 교체와 통합 외에 답 있나
editorial.body: (사설) 국민의힘이 지방선거와 재보궐 선거에서 패배한 원인은 불법계엄과 탄핵 반성 부족, 지도부 무능, 당내 분열에 있다. 오세훈 승리 등 일부 선전에도 지도부 책임론이 부각되며, 국민의힘 미래는 지도부 교체와 당내 통합, 혁신에 달려 있다. |
summary: 국민의힘, 지방선거 패배의 원인은 불법계엄과 탄핵 반성 부족
지도부 무능과 당내 분열이 선거 참패를 초래했다
지도부 교체와 당내 통합만이 당의 미래를 살릴 수 있다
editorial.pullQuote: 국민의힘 미래는 지도부 교체와 당내 통합, 혁신에 달려 있다
editorial.stance: 국민의힘은 지도부 책임을 인정하고 근본적 개혁을 요구한다
editorial.titleTr: People Power Party Faces Pressure: Is Leadership Reshuffle and Unity the Only Way Out?
summaryTr: People Power Party attributes local election defeat to lack of reflection on illegal martial law and impeachment
Leadership incompetence and internal party division led to election rout
Only leadership change and internal party unity can save the party's future
editorial.bodyTr: The Democratic People's Power Party's defeats in the local elections and by-elections stem from the illegal martial law declaration, insufficient reflection on impeachment, leadership incompetence, and internal party divisions. Despite some victories such as Oh Se-hoon's win, criticism of leadership responsibility has become prominent, and the future of the Democratic People's Power Party depends on replacing its leadership, achieving party unity, and driving innovation.

leanScore.label: Lean progressive
leanScore.rationale: 불법계엄과 탄핵 문제를 중점적으로 비판하며 현 보수 정부 지도부의 책임을 강조하는 진보적 프레이밍

editorial.title: [사설] 사상 최대 경상 흑자인데 환율 최악, 위험한 기현상
editorial.body: 사상 최대 경상 흑자인데 환율 최악, 위험한 기현상 반도체 수퍼 사이클에 힘입은 막대한 경상수지 흑자에도 불구하고 원·달러 환율이 1500원대에서 내려오지 않는 기현상이 장기화하고 있다. 어제 환율은 전날보다 9.4원 오른 1539.1원으로 마감했다. 글로벌 금
editorial.pullQuote: 사상 최대 경상 흑자인데 환율 최악, 위험한 기현상
summary: 사상 최대 경상흑자에도 원-달러 환율이 1500원대 고착된 기현상
반도체 수퍼사이클 호황이 환율 약세 현상을 설명하지 못함
경제 펀더멘털과 환율의 괴리, 위험 신호로 작용
editorial.stance: 경제 기초체력은 강한데 환율 약세가 지속되는 것은 구조적 문제로 봐야 한다
editorial.titleTr: [Editorial] Record Current Account Surplus Yet Worst-Ever Exchange Rate: A Dangerous Paradox
summaryTr: A puzzling phenomenon: the won-dollar exchange rate remains stuck in the 1,500-won range despite record current account surplus
The semiconductor super-cycle boom fails to explain the weakness in the exchange rate
The disconnect between economic fundamentals and the exchange rate serves as a warning signal
editorial.bodyTr: Record-high current account surplus yet worst-ever exchange rate, a dangerous anomaly persists. Despite massive current account surpluses buoyed by the semiconductor super cycle, the anomalous phenomenon of the won-dollar exchange rate refusing to fall below the 1,500-won level is prolonging. Yesterday's exchange rate closed at 1,539.1 won, up 9.4 won from the previous day. With the global financial...

editorials.title: [사설] 野 찍은 국민에 “내란” 비난, 상식 밖 인물이 ‘공정’위원장
editorials.body: 野 찍은 국민에 내란 비난, 상식 밖 인물이 공정위원장 주병기 공정거래위원장이 지난 4일 서울시장 선거와 관련해 시민의 권리 행사가 이렇게 돈의 질서와 일치한다는 사실이 씁쓸하다는 글을 올렸다. 강남권에서 오세훈 시장 지지가 상대적으로 높은 것을 겨냥한 것으로
editorials.pullQuote: 시민의 권리 행사가 이렇게 돈의 질서와 일치한다는 사실이 씁쓸하다
summary: 공정위원장이 野 지지 국민을 내란으로 비난했다.
상식 밖의 인물이 공정위 수장이 된 현실을 지적한다.
시민의 권리 행사까지 정치적으로 평가하는 태도를 비판한다.
editorials.stance: 정치적 중립성을 상실한 공정위원장의 부적절한 발언을 강하게 비판하는 입장
editorials.titleTr: Editorial: Calling Citizens Who Backed Opposition "Traitors," An Unconventional Figure Heads "Fairness" Commission
summaryTr: The Fair Trade Commission chairman criticized opposition supporters as seditious.
It points out the reality that an unconventional figure has become the head of the Fair Trade Commission.
It criticizes the attitude of politically evaluating even citizens' exercise of their rights.
editorials.bodyTr:
Translation

Common Sense Derailed: Accusations of Insurrection Against Voters Who Made Their Choice, an Unsuitable Figure as Fair Trade Commission Chair

Fair Trade Commission Chair Ju Byung-gi posted on the 4th regarding the Seoul mayoral election, expressing bitterness that citizens' exercise of their rights happens to align so perfectly with the order of money. The post appeared to target the relatively high level of support for Mayor Oh Se-hoon in the Gangnam area.

leanScore.label: Lean progressive
leanScore.rationale: 보수 진영 인물(주병기 공정위원장)을 '상식 밖'이라 비판하고 정치적 중립성 위반을 강하게 지적하며, 야당 지지 국민을 '내란'으로 부르는 것을 비판하는 검찰·정부 권력에 대한 견제 프레임

editorials.title: [사설] 조정식 새 국회의장, 폭주 말라는 선거 민심 받들길
editorials.body: 조정식 새 국회의장, 폭주 말라는 선거 민심 받들길 민주당 원내대표가 지방선거 뒤 민의를 무겁게 받아들인다고 했다. 이어 민생을 위해서라면 야당과 협치에도 적극 나서겠다고 했다. 국회는 5일 22대 국회 하반기 의장으로 6선인 조정식 민주당 의원을 선출했다. .
editorials.pullQuote: 민생을 위해서라면 야당과 협치에도 적극 나서겠다고 했다.
summary: 조정식 민주당 의원이 22대 국회 하반기 의장으로 선출됐다.
민주당은 지방선거 결과를 받아들이고 민생 중심으로 움직이겠다고 했다.
야당과의 협치로 국회의 폭주를 자제해야 한다는 선거 민심을 반영해야 한다.
editorials.stance: 새 의장은 지방선거 민심을 존중하며 여야 협력으로 국정 운영의 안정성을 도모해야 한다.
editorials.titleTr: Editorial: New National Assembly Speaker Jo Jung-sik Must Heed Electoral Mandate Against Recklessness
summaryTr: Democratic Party Rep. Jo Jeong-sik was elected as Speaker of the National Assembly for the second half of the 22nd Parliament.
The Democratic Party said it will accept the results of the local elections and focus on improving people's livelihoods.
It must reflect the electoral will that the runaway National Assembly should be restrained through cooperation with opposition parties.
editorials.bodyTr:
The Democratic Party floor leader has said he will take the people's will seriously following the local elections. He added that he would actively pursue cooperation with the opposition party if it is for the people's livelihood. On the 5th, the National Assembly elected six-term Democratic Party lawmaker Jo Jung-sik as speaker for the second half of the 22nd National Assembly.

The newly elected Speaker Jo Jung-sik should heed the electoral mandate calling for restraint. The Democratic Party floor leader has stated that he will take the people's will seriously in the wake of the local elections, and that he will actively pursue bipartisan cooperation for the sake of people's livelihoods. The National Assembly elected six-term Democratic Party member Jo Jung-sik as speaker for the second half of the 22nd National Assembly on the 5th.

leanScore.label: Center
leanScore.rationale: 여야 협치와 국회의 '폭주' 자제를 강조하며 특정 정파의 정책이나 이념을 지지하지 않고 민심 반영과 안정성을 중립적으로 촉구하는 기사.

editorial.title: [사설] 비밀 유출 논란 자초한 국방장관 발언
editorial.body: 입력 종합 26면 안규백 국방부 장관이 지난달 31일(현지시간) 제23차 아시아 안보회의(샹그릴라 대화)가 열리고 있는 싱가포르 샹그릴라 호텔에서 취재진과 만나 현안에 대해 설명하고 있다. [연합뉴스] 전시작전통제권(전작권) 전환을 위한 한·미 양국의 평가 수준을 공개한 안규백 국방부 장관의 발언이 비밀 유출 논란에 휩싸였다. 안 장관은 지난달 31일 싱가포르 출장 중 “한·미 양국은 2020년에 전작권 전환 조건의 94%가 이미 충족됐다고 합의한 것을 비롯해 우리의 능력에 대해 충분히 설명했다”고 밝혔다. 이날 아시아안보회의(샹그릴라 대화)에 참석한 미국 의원들을 만나 한국군의 전작권 행사 능력을 설명하고 협조를 요청했다는 것이다. 한·미는 조건이 갖춰지면 전작권을 한국군에 이양하기로 하고, 구체적인 매뉴얼을 만들어 평가를 진행 중이다. 하지만 구체적인 평가 항목이나 이행 수준은 한·미 연합의 비밀로 간주하고 있다. 자칫 한·미 연합 전력 수준과 방어 태세가 북한 등에 공개될 것을 우려해서다. 그런데 국방부 수장이 나서 양국 군 당국이 공개를 꺼려 온 94%라는 구체적인 수치를 언급한 것이다. 논란이 일자 국방부는 “조속한 전작권 회복과 관련한 국민의 알권리 보장과 이해를 돕기 위해 개략적인 수치를 제시한 것”이라며 “구체적인 내용을 적시한 게 아니어서 한·미 연합 비밀에 해당하지 않는다”고 해명했다. 하지만 국방부는 지난해 전작권 전환 평가 결과에 대한 국회의 자료 제출 요구에 “(전작권과 관련한) 평가 항목 및 결과 등 구체적인 사항은 한·미 연합의 비밀로 답변이 제한된다”며 비공개 입장을 고수한 바 있다. 전작권 평가 추정이 가능한 자료를 공개하지 않겠다는 뜻이다. 군 당국이 지금까지 7년 넘게 국회에 관련 내용을 설명하지 않은 것은 사안의 민감성을 방증한다. 미국은 지난 3월 정동영 통일부 장관의 북한 구성시 우라늄 농축시설 존재 언급을 이유로 아직까지 북핵 동향 관련 정보 제공을 중단하고 있다. 한·미 동맹에 균열 징후가 이어지고 있는 만큼 책임 있는 당국자라면 단어 하나에도 신중을 기해 발언하는 것이 맞다. 한·미는 어제부터 1박2일 동안 한국군의 핵추진잠수함 보유와 우라늄 농축 등과 관련한 협의를 시작했다. 현 정부가 역점을 두고 있는 전작권 환수나 핵추진잠수함 보유 등은 결국 한·미 동맹의 튼튼한 신뢰가 기반이 돼야 순조롭게 진행될 수 있다는 점을 분명히 인식할 필요가 있다.
editorial.pullQuote: 책임 있는 당국자라면 단어 하나에도 신중을 기해 발언하는 것이 맞다.
summary: 국방장관이 전작권 전환 평가 수준 94%를 공개해 비밀 유출 논란 초래했다.
군 당국은 한·미 연합 비밀이라며 국회에 자료 제출을 거부해왔다.
한·미 동맹 신뢰가 기반되어야 전작권 환수 등이 순조롭게 진행된다.
editorial.stance: 정부는 민감한 안보 사안에서 일관성 있고 신중한 정보 관리로 한·미 신뢰를 지켜야 한다.
editorial.titleTr: Defense Minister's Remarks Invite Classified Information Leak Controversy
summaryTr: The defense minister disclosed a 94% readiness assessment level for the transition of wartime operational control, triggering controversy over secret information leaks.
Military authorities have refused to submit documents to the National Assembly, citing the information as a South Korea-U.S. alliance secret.
The smooth progress of regaining wartime operational control depends on building trust in the South Korea-U.S. alliance.
editorial.bodyTr:
Defense Minister An Kyou-paik has become embroiled in a controversy over leaked classified information after publicly disclosing the assessment level between South Korea and the United States regarding the transfer of operational control. During a business trip to Singapore last month, Minister An stated, "South Korea and the United States agreed that 94 percent of the conditions for the transfer of wartime operational control (OPCON) had already been met as of 2020, and we have sufficiently explained our capabilities." He reportedly met with U.S. lawmakers attending the Shangri-La Dialogue in Singapore to brief them on the South Korean military's ability to exercise operational control and to request cooperation.

Seoul and Washington have agreed to transfer operational control to the South Korean military once conditions are met, and are currently conducting assessments based on detailed manuals. However, the specific evaluation criteria and implementation levels are classified as a South Korea-U.S. joint military secret due to concerns that operational capability levels and defense postures could be exposed to North Korea and other adversaries.

Yet the head of the Defense Ministry publicly mentioned the specific figure of 94 percent, which military authorities on both sides have been reluctant to disclose. When controversy ensued, the Defense Ministry explained that it had presented only a rough figure to guarantee public awareness and understanding regarding the early recovery of operational control, and that it did not mention specific details and therefore did not constitute a breach of South Korea-U.S. joint military secrets. However, the ministry previously maintained a position of non-disclosure when rejecting the National Assembly's request for materials regarding last year's OPCON transfer assessment results, stating that responses were restricted as evaluation criteria and results constituted South Korea-U.S. joint military secrets. This amounts to refusing to disclose information from which OPCON assessments could be inferred.

The military authorities' refusal to explain related matters to the National Assembly for over seven years demonstrates the sensitivity of the issue. The United States has suspended the provision of information related to North Korean nuclear developments since March, citing Unification Minister Chung Dong-young's remarks about the existence of uranium enrichment facilities in a unified Korean peninsula. Given the continuing signs of fissures in the South Korea-U.S. alliance, responsible government officials must exercise caution in every word they speak. Starting yesterday, South Korea and the United States began one-and-a-half-day consultations regarding the South Korean military's acquisition of nuclear-powered submarines and uranium enrichment. It must be clearly recognized that priority initiatives of the current government—such as the recovery of operational control and the acquisition of nuclear-powered submarines—can only proceed smoothly when built on a strong foundation of trust in the South Korea-U.S. alliance.

leanScore.label: Lean conservative
leanScore.rationale: 국방장관의 비밀 유출을 비판하며 한·미 동맹의 신뢰와 안보 체계 유지를 강조하는 관료적·보수적 입장으로, 정부 신뢰성과 한·미 연합 기밀 유지를 핵심 프레임으로 삼음

editorial.title: Mandate without excuses
editorial.body: In local elections, voters granted power but demand results, restraint, reform Election victories are often called mandates. In practice, they function more like mortgages, granting power today while billing governments later. South Korea’s June 3 local elections have left little doubt about who now owns the country's political future. One year after taking office, President Lee Jae Myung's administration has extended its reach across much of the country, with the ruling Democratic Party of Korea capturing 12 of 16 major mayoral and gubernatorial posts. Having already secured the presidency and the National Assembly, it now commands most of the nation’s local political machinery, completing an extraordinary concentration of political power. Yet the election results were not quite the blank check they may appear to be. The main opposition People Power Party retained the Seoul mayoralty, with Oh Se-hoon winning a historic fifth term, and held on to several conservative strongholds. In concurrent parliamentary by-elections, the ruling party surrendered four seats it had previously controlled. Voters strengthened the government, but also signaled that dominance is not consensus. The turnout itself carried a message. At 61.0 percent, participation reached its highest level in a local election since 1995. Such engagement is usually hailed as democratic vitality. Yet the forces driving voters to the polls were less about local governance than national confrontation. The ruling party framed the election as a final judgment on the remnants of the period under former President Yoon Suk Yeol, while conservatives mobilized against the prospect of one-party dominance. The result was a highly energized electorate, but not necessarily a more deliberative one. Indeed, some of the campaign’s weaknesses were hard to miss. Election-law violations rose sharply. Hundreds of candidates ran unopposed in regional strongholds, leaving millions of voters without meaningful choices. What should have been a contest over regional development became another chapter in national political warfare. That failure belongs to both parties. The ruling camp spent much of the campaign focused on settling historical and judicial scores. The opposition remained trapped in arguments about its own past, unable to persuade voters that it had learned from the collapse of the Yoon presidency. Neither side devoted sufficient attention to questions that matter beyond Seoul: How to revive struggling provincial economies, spread the gains from export growth and prepare regions for future-oriented industries. The election also exposed an institutional weakness that should concern all democrats. Ballot shortages at multiple polling stations in Seoul disrupted voting and forced temporary suspensions. In one district, voting continued long after exit polls had been broadcast. Protesters later blocked the transfer of ballot boxes amid allegations of fraud. The National Election Commission has rejected calls for a revote, but the larger lesson remains. Administrative incompetence is fertile ground for conspiracy theories. Now comes the more difficult phase. South Korea enters a rare two-year period without a nationwide election before the 2028 parliamentary vote. In a political system consumed by permanent campaigning, such opportunities are uncommon. The government can no longer plausibly blame obstruction, gridlock or divided authority for policy failures. Korea’s potential growth continues to decline. Demographic pressures are intensifying. Wealth and opportunity are increasingly unevenly distributed. The recent boom in exports and equity markets provides relief, but cannot cure deeper structural weaknesses. The local elections stripped away many of the political excuses governments typically enjoy. That may prove their most enduring consequence. The Lee government spent its first year defining ambitions. Its second will be judged by execution. Economic polarization, slowing potential growth and labor-market rigidities will not be solved by slogans or retrospective battles. The voters who dismantled much of the conservative opposition were not endorsing permanent one-party rule. They were demanding results. Governments rarely receive a clearer opportunity. The bill for this political mortgage will come due soon enough. editorial.pullQuote: Administrative incompetence is fertile ground for conspiracy theories.
summary: South Korea's ruling party won decisively in local elections but voters signaled dominance isn't consensus.
Both major parties wasted the campaign on national politics instead of addressing regional development needs.
With no elections until 2028, the government can no longer blame obstruction—execution now determines legacy.
editorial.stance: Election victories grant power but demand accountability; South Korea's government must deliver results, not excuses.
summaryTr: 한국 여당이 지방선거에서 압승을 거뒀지만 유권자들은 압도적 우위가 합의를 의미하지는 않는다는 신호를 보냈다.
양대 정당은 지역 개발 필요성을 다루는 대신 캠프 기간을 국가 정치에 낭비했다.
2028년까지 선거가 없어진 만큼 정부는 더 이상 견제를 탓할 수 없으며, 이제 실행력이 유산을 결정한다.
editorial.bodyTr:
지방선거에서 유권자들은 권력을 부여했지만 실적과 절제, 개혁을 요구했다

선거 승리는 종종 위임이라고 불린다. 실제로는 오늘의 권력을 부여하면서 나중에 정부에 청구서를 보내는 저당처럼 작동한다. 한국의 6월 3일 지방선거는 누가 이제 국가의 정치 미래를 소유하고 있는지에 대해 의심의 여지가 없다. 이준석 대통령 행정부는 취임 1년 만에 국가 대부분으로 영향력을 확대했으며, 여당인 민주당이 16개 광역 시장 및 도지사 직책 중 12개를 확보했다. 이미 대통령직과 국회를 확보한 데 이어 이제는 국가의 대부분의 지방 정치 기구를 장악하면서 비상한 정치권력의 집중을 완성했다. 그러나 선거 결과는 보이는 것만큼 완전한 백지수표는 아니었다. 야당인 국민의힘은 오세훈이 역사적인 5번째 임기를 획득하며 서울시장직을 유지했으며, 보수 강지 여러 곳을 지켜냈다. 동시에 치러진 국회 보궐선거에서 여당은 이전에 장악했던 4개 의석을 넘겨주었다. 유권자들은 정부를 강화했지만 동시에 지배가 합의가 아님을 신호했다. 투표율 자체가 메시지를 담고 있었다. 61.0%의 투표율은 1995년 이후 지방선거 중 가장 높은 참여도에 도달했다. 이러한 참여는 보통 민주적 활력으로 찬양된다. 그러나 유권자들을 투표소로 몰아낸 힘은 지방 통치보다는 국가적 대립에 관한 것이었다. 여당은 선거를 윤석열 전 대통령 시기의 잔재에 대한 최종 판단으로 프레이밍했고, 보수진영은 한 정당의 지배 전망에 맞서 동원되었다. 결과는 에너지가 넘치는 유권자였지만 반드시 더 신중한 유권자는 아니었다. 실제로 캠프의 일부 약점은 놓치기 어려웠다. 선거법 위반이 급증했다. 수백 명의 후보자가 지역 강지에서 무투표로 당선되었으며, 수백만 명의 유권자는 의미 있는 선택지 없이 남겨졌다. 지역 개발에 대한 경쟁이어야 할 것이 국가 정치 전쟁의 또 다른 장이 되었다. 이러한 실패는 양당 모두에게 책임이 있다. 여권은 캠프 대부분을 역사적, 사법적 점수 결산에 집중했다. 야권은 자신의 과거에 대한 논쟁에 갇혀, 윤석열 정부 붕괴로부터 교훈을 얻었다는 것을 유권자에게 설득하지 못했다. 양측 모두 서울을 넘어 중요한 문제들에 충분한 주의를 기울이지 않았다. 어떻게 지치는 지방 경제를 되살릴 것인가, 수출 성장의 이득을 어떻게 확산시킬 것인가, 지역들을 미래지향적 산업을 위해 어떻게 준비시킬 것인가.

선거는 또한 모든 민주주의자들을 우려하게 해야 할 제도적 약점을 노출했다. 서울의 여러 투표소에서 투표용지 부족으로 투표가 중단되고 일시 중단을 강요했다. 한 지역구에서는 출구조사가 방송된 후에도 투표가 계속되었다. 시위대는 나중에 부정행위 의혹 속에 투표함 이동을 막았다. 중앙선거위원회는 재투표 촉구를 거부했지만, 더 큰 교훈이 남아있다. 행정적 무능함은 음모론의 옥토다.

이제 더 어려운 단계가 시작된다. 한국은 2028년 국회의원 선거 전까지 국가 차원의 선거가 없는 드문 2년의 기간에 진입한다. 영구적 선거운동에 빠진 정치 체제에서 이러한 기회는 드물다. 정부는 더 이상 정책 실패의 이유로 방해, 교착상태 또는 분산된 권한을 그럴듯하게 탓할 수 없다. 한국의 잠재 성장률은 계속 하락하고 있다. 인구 압박이 심화되고 있다. 부와 기회가 점점 더 불균등하게 분배되고 있다. 최근의 수출과 주식시장 붐은 완화를 제공하지만 더 깊은 구조적 약점을 치유할 수 없다. 지방선거는 정부가 보통 누리는 많은 정치적 핑계를 벗겨냈다. 이것이 가장 오래 지속되는 결과가 될 수 있다. 이준석 정부는 첫 해를 야심 정의에 보냈다. 두 번째 해는 실행으로 판단받을 것이다. 경제 양극화, 낮아지는 잠재 성장률, 노동시장 경직성은 슬로건이나 회고적 전투로 해결되지 않을 것이다. 보수 야권을 많이 해체한 유권자들은 항구적 일당 지배를 지지하지 않았다. 그들은 실적을 요구했다. 정부가 더 명확한 기회를 받는 경우는 드물다. 이 정치적 저당의 청구서는 곧 만기가 될 것이다.

editorials.title: Mandate without excuses
editorials.body: In local elections, voters granted power but demand results, restraint, reform Election victories are often called mandates. In practice, they function more like mortgages, granting power today while billing governments later. South Korea’s June 3 local elections have left little doubt about who now owns the country's political future. One year after taking office, President Lee Jae Myung's administration has extended its reach across much of the country, with the ruling Democratic Party of Korea capturing 12 of 16 major mayoral and gubernatorial posts. Having already secured the presidency and the National Assembly, it now commands most of the nation’s local political machinery, completing an extraordinary concentration of political power. Yet the election results were not quite the blank check they may appear to be. The main opposition People Power Party retained the Seoul mayoralty, with Oh Se-hoon winning a historic fifth term, and held on to several conservative strongholds. In concurrent parliamentary by-elections, the ruling party surrendered four seats it had previously controlled. Voters strengthened the government, but also signaled that dominance is not consensus. The turnout itself carried a message. At 61.0 percent, participation reached its highest level in a local election since 1995. Such engagement is usually hailed as democratic vitality. Yet the forces driving voters to the polls were less about local governance than national confrontation. The ruling party framed the election as a final judgment on the remnants of the period under former President Yoon Suk Yeol, while conservatives mobilized against the prospect of one-party dominance. The result was a highly energized electorate, but not necessarily a more deliberative one. Indeed, some of the campaign’s weaknesses were hard to miss. Election-law violations rose sharply. Hundreds of candidates ran unopposed in regional strongholds, leaving millions of voters without meaningful choices. What should have been a contest over regional development became another chapter in national political warfare. That failure belongs to both parties. The ruling camp spent much of the campaign focused on settling historical and judicial scores. The opposition remained trapped in arguments about its own past, unable to persuade voters that it had learned from the collapse of the Yoon presidency. Neither side devoted sufficient attention to questions that matter beyond Seoul: How to revive struggling provincial economies, spread the gains from export growth and prepare regions for future-oriented industries. The election also exposed an institutional weakness that should concern all democrats. Ballot shortages at multiple polling stations in Seoul disrupted voting and forced temporary suspensions. In one district, voting continued long after exit polls had been broadcast. Protesters later blocked the transfer of ballot boxes amid allegations of fraud. The National Election Commission has rejected calls for a revote, but the larger lesson remains. Administrative incompetence is fertile ground for conspiracy theories. Now comes the more difficult phase. South Korea enters a rare two-year period without a nationwide election before the 2028 parliamentary vote. In a political system consumed by permanent campaigning, such opportunities are uncommon. The government can no longer plausibly blame obstruction, gridlock or divided authority for policy failures. Korea’s potential growth continues to decline. Demographic pressures are intensifying. Wealth and opportunity are increasingly unevenly distributed. The recent boom in exports and equity markets provides relief, but cannot cure deeper structural weaknesses. The local elections stripped away many of the political excuses governments typically enjoy. That may prove their most enduring consequence. The Lee government spent its first year defining ambitions. Its second will be judged by execution. Economic polarization, slowing potential growth and labor-market rigidities will not be solved by slogans or retrospective battles. The voters who dismantled much of the conservative opposition were not endorsing permanent one-party rule. They were demanding results. Governments rarely receive a clearer opportunity. The bill for this political mortgage will come due soon enough. summary: South Korea's ruling party won decisively in local elections but voters signaled dominance isn't consensus.
Both major parties wasted the campaign on national politics instead of addressing regional development needs.
With no elections until 2028, the government can no longer blame obstruction—execution now determines legacy.
editorials.stance: Election victories grant power but demand accountability; South Korea's government must deliver results, not excuses.
summaryTr: 한국 여당이 지방선거에서 압승을 거뒀지만 유권자들은 압도적 우위가 합의를 의미하지는 않는다는 신호를 보냈다.
양대 정당은 지역 개발 필요성을 다루는 대신 캠프 기간을 국가 정치에 낭비했다.
2028년까지 선거가 없어진 만큼 정부는 더 이상 견제를 탓할 수 없으며, 이제 실행력이 유산을 결정한다.

editorial.title: [사설] 무효 108만표 나온 교육감 선거, 개편 논의 시작해야
editorial.body: 전국 16개 시도교육감 선거에서 무효표 108만8403표(5일 오후 6시 기준)가 나왔다. 이번 시도지사 선거 무효표 43만4300표의 두 배가 넘는다. 2022년 교육감 선거 때 90만3249표였던 것과 비교해도 많다. 교육감 선거는 가뜩이나 ‘깜깜이 선거’인데, 이

editorials.titleTr: Editorial: Ballot Shortage Inquiry Must Lead to Overhaul of Election Commission
summaryTr: The chief of the National Election Commission, Roh Tae-ak, resigned over responsibility for the ballot shortage crisis.
During the June 3 local elections, an incident of voting rights violation occurred.
Public distrust in the electoral system is intensifying.
editorials.bodyTr: Noh Tae-ak, chairman of the Central Election Management Commission, resigned on the 5th, taking responsibility for the "voting ballot shortage crisis" that occurred during the June 3 local elections. He issued a public apology, saying "an unacceptable situation that violated the people's precious right to vote has occurred." This incident has shaken public trust in the electoral system, which is at the core of democracy.

editorial.titleTr: Cabinet Reshuffle Imminent...Personnel Appointments to Support Inclusive Governance
summaryTr: Prime Minister Kim Min-seok's resignation expected to lead to successor nomination within 7 days
Cabinet reshuffle in second year of term signals timing for realigning government leadership
Need to overcome Seoul mayoral election defeat and adjust operational direction
editorial.bodyTr: Prime Minister Kim Min-seok has reportedly submitted his resignation, with the appointment of his successor expected as early as seven days from now. With the administration now entering its second year in office, this is clearly an opportune moment to reorganize the government lineup. The cabinet reshuffle can serve as an occasion to refresh the party and government atmosphere, which has been dampened by defeat in the Seoul mayoral election. We hope this will be used as an opportunity to adjust the direction and style of state affairs management. Among candidates for the next prime minister is Kang Hun-sik, chief of the presidential secretariat...

It has been seven months since his last "deep friendship meeting" with Samsung Electronics Chairman Lee Jae-yong and Hyundai Motor Group Chairman Chung Euisun last year. The consensus view is that this visit to Korea represents Nvidia's strategic positioning of the country as a partner in "physical AI." editorials.titleTr: Xi Jinping's First North Korea Visit in 7 Years Stokes Fears of Tighter China-Russia-North Korea Alliance
summaryTr: Xi Jinping visits North Korea for first time in 7 years...strengthening China-North Korea-Russia solidarity
State visit to North Korea right after China-Russia summit reveals intent to solidify three-nation alliance
Deepening strategic cooperation with Russia while maintaining negotiation channels with the United States
editorials.bodyTr: Chinese President Xi Jinping is visiting North Korea for the first time in seven years. Xi's visit marks his first trip to North Korea since June 2019 and the second since Kim Jong-un came to power. Notably, this visit follows immediately after the China-Russia summit and comes on the heels of the three leaders' attendance at China's Victory Day commemoration last September, clearly demonstrating strengthened coordination among North Korea, China, and Russia. China is maintaining negotiating channels with the United States while bolstering strategic cooperation with Russia and reinforcing its influence over North Korea...
